name: Clinical Guidance Expansion Pack
description: Supplemental local clinical guidance topics for common specialist, safety, and symptom-review pathways.
reviewStatus: local pack requires clinician review before production medical use

records.title: Chest Pressure, Exertion, and Cardiac Warning Pattern
records.summary: Organizes chest pressure questions around exertion, rest symptoms, sweating, nausea, shortness of breath, radiation to jaw or arm, and symptom duration.
records.safetyNotes: Active chest pressure, breathlessness, fainting, or sweating should be treated as urgent real-world care needs.
whatToTrack: When the chest symptom started, whether it came on at rest or with exertion, and whether it spreads to jaw, arm, or back.
Associated symptoms such as sweating, nausea, shortness of breath, dizziness, or fainting.
Relevant BP, pulse, prior heart history, and medicines already taken.
careQuestions: Which details make this chest symptom more concerning for urgent cardiac review?
What should be documented before speaking to a clinician or emergency team?
What recent readings or history should be kept ready?
precautions: Do not continue routine app review if active chest pressure or severe shortness of breath is happening now.
Do not self-diagnose angina versus heart attack from symptom education alone.

records.title: Infection Deterioration and Sepsis Warning Questions
records.summary: Creates a structured warning-sign review for infection deterioration using fever or low temperature, confusion, weakness, breathing rate, hydration, and urine output context.
records.safetyNotes: Confusion, severe weakness, blue lips, breathing trouble, very low blood pressure, or rapidly worsening illness needs urgent medical attention.

records.title: Sleep Apnea, Snoring, and Daytime Fatigue Review
records.summary: Helps organize snoring, breathing pauses during sleep, unrefreshing sleep, morning headache, weight, and daytime fatigue before clinician follow-up.
records.safetyNotes: Severe daytime sleepiness affecting driving, major breathing concern, or chest symptoms requires direct real-world review.

records.title: Thyroid Medication Timing and Lab Follow-up
records.summary: Organizes thyroid medicine and hormone-lab questions using dose timing, fasting routine, symptoms, pregnancy context, and repeat-test intervals.
records.safetyNotes: Palpitations, fainting, severe weakness, or major swelling need direct clinician review instead of routine timing discussion.

records.title: Chronic Kidney Monitoring and Hydration Checklist
records.summary: Creates a kidney-monitoring checklist using lab trend, swelling, urine changes, BP, diabetes, hydration, and medicine-safety context.
records.safetyNotes: Severe swelling, no urine output, confusion, vomiting, or rapidly worsening symptoms should not wait for routine kidney review.

records.title: Wound, Dressing, and Infection Check
records.summary: Structures wound and dressing questions around redness, discharge, swelling, pain, fever, dressing changes, and procedure date.
records.safetyNotes: Rapidly spreading redness, heavy bleeding, severe pain, fever, or wound opening needs prompt real-world assessment.

records.title: Urinary Burning, Fever, and Flank Pain Organizer
records.summary: Organizes urinary questions using burning, urgency, fever, flank pain, blood in urine, hydration, pregnancy context, and past infection history.
records.safetyNotes: Fever with flank pain, vomiting, pregnancy, confusion, or severe weakness needs prompt medical review.

records.title: Pregnancy Blood Pressure, Swelling, and Headache Warnings
records.summary: Creates a maternal warning review for headache, swelling, BP, vision changes, reduced fetal movement, abdominal pain, and postpartum concerns.
records.safetyNotes: Pregnancy or postpartum headache with high BP, seizure, heavy bleeding, severe pain, or reduced fetal movement needs urgent real-world care.
